260: function buildHeuristicFindings(
261:   review: ReviewRunResponse,
262:   deploymentEvents: Array<{ eventType: string; payload: unknown; seq: number }>
263: ): ReviewFinding[] {
264:   return deploymentEvents.flatMap<ReviewFinding>((event, index) => {
265:     const eventPayload = asRecord(event.payload);
266:     if (event.eventType === 'deployment_validation_tool_missing') {
267:       const step = typeof eventPayload.step === 'string' ? eventPayload.step : 'validation';
268:       return [
269:         {
270:           severity: 'medium',
271:           category: 'logic',
272:           passType: 'single',
273:           locations: [{ filePath: 'deployment', startLine: null, endLine: null }],
274:           description:
275:             typeof eventPayload.message === 'string'
276:               ? eventPayload.message
277:               : `Validation tool missing for ${step} in runtime.`,
278:           suggestedFix: `Install the required ${step} validation tool in the deployment runtime or disable that validation step explicitly.`,
279:         },
280:       ];
281:     }
282:     if (event.eventType === 'validation_skipped') {
283:       const step = typeof eventPayload.step === 'string' ? eventPayload.step : 'validation';
284:       return [
285:         {
286:           severity: 'low',
287:           category: 'style',
288:           passType: 'single',
289:           locations: [{ filePath: 'deployment', startLine: null, endLine: null }],
290:           description: `Nimbus skipped ${step} validation while preparing this deployment review.`,
291:           suggestedFix: `Run the ${step} validation in the deployment path or document why it is intentionally skipped.`,
292:         },
293:       ];
294:     }
295:     if (event.eventType === 'deployment_toolchain_unknown_fallback') {
296:       return [
297:         {
298:           severity: 'low',
299:           category: 'style',
300:           passType: 'single',
301:           locations: [{ filePath: 'deployment', startLine: null, endLine: null }],
302:           description: 'Deployment completed after a toolchain fallback, which may hide package-manager-specific issues.',
303:           suggestedFix: 'Declare an explicit package manager and lockfile so future deploys and reviews use deterministic tooling.',
304:         },
305:       ];
306:     }
307:     return [];
308:   });
309: }

311: function buildEvidence(
312:   deploymentEvents: Array<{ eventType: string; payload: unknown; seq: number }>,
313:   deployment: { deployedUrl: string | null },
314:   resultArtifact: Record<string, unknown>,
315:   includeValidationEvidence: boolean,
316:   agentEvidence?: ReviewEvidenceItem | null
317: ): ReviewEvidenceItem[] {
318:   const evidence: ReviewEvidenceItem[] = includeValidationEvidence
319:     ? deploymentEvents
320:         .filter((event) => {
321:           return [
322:             'validation_started',
323:             'validation_skipped',
324:             'deployment_validation_tool_missing',
325:             'deployment_provider_created',
326:             'deployment_provider_status',
327:             'deployment_succeeded',
328:           ].includes(event.eventType);
329:         })
330:         .map((event) => ({
331:           id: `ev_${event.seq}`,
332:           type: event.eventType,
333:           label: event.eventType.replaceAll('_', ' '),
334:           status: statusFromEventType(event.eventType, asRecord(event.payload)),
335:           metadata: asRecord(event.payload),
336:         }))
337:     : [];
338: 
339:   if (includeValidationEvidence && deployment.deployedUrl) {
340:     evidence.push({
341:       id: 'ev_deployed_url',
342:       type: 'deploy_probe',
343:       label: 'Deployed URL present',
344:       status: 'passed',
345:       metadata: { url: deployment.deployedUrl },
346:     });
347:   }
348:   if (
349:     includeValidationEvidence &&
350:     (typeof resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey === 'string' || typeof resultArtifact.sourceSnapshotSha256 === 'string')
351:   ) {
352:     evidence.push({
353:       id: 'ev_artifact',
354:       type: 'artifact',
355:       label: 'Deployment artifact recorded',
356:       status: 'info',
357:       metadata: resultArtifact,
358:     });
359:   }
360:   if (agentEvidence) {
361:     evidence.push(agentEvidence);
362:   }
363: 
364:   return evidence;
365: }

367: function deriveRiskLevel(findings: ReviewFinding[], fallback: ReviewSeverity = 'low'): ReviewSeverity {
368:   if (findings.some((finding) => finding.severity === 'critical')) {
369:     return 'critical';
370:   }
371:   if (findings.some((finding) => finding.severity === 'high')) {
372:     return 'high';
373:   }
374:   if (findings.some((finding) => finding.severity === 'medium')) {
375:     return 'medium';
376:   }
377:   if (findings.some((finding) => finding.severity === 'low')) {
378:     return 'low';
379:   }
380:   return fallback;
381: }
382: 
383: function deriveRecommendation(findings: ReviewFinding[]): ReviewRecommendation {
384:   const riskLevel = deriveRiskLevel(findings);
385:   if (riskLevel === 'critical' || riskLevel === 'high') {
386:     return 'request_changes';
387:   }
388:   if (riskLevel === 'medium' || riskLevel === 'low') {
389:     return findings.length > 0 ? 'comment' : 'approve';
390:   }
391:   return 'approve';
392: }

394: function sanitizeIntentBlock(intent: {
395:   goal: string | null;
396:   constraints: string[];
397:   decisions: string[];
398: }): { goal: string | null; constraints: string[]; decisions: string[] } {
399:   return {
400:     goal: redactReviewText(intent.goal),
401:     constraints: intent.constraints.map((item) => redactReviewText(item) ?? '').filter(Boolean),
402:     decisions: intent.decisions.map((item) => redactReviewText(item) ?? '').filter(Boolean),
403:   };
404: }

406: function parseChangedPathsFromDiff(patch: string): string[] {
407:   const paths = new Set<string>();
408:   for (const line of patch.split('\n')) {
409:     if (!line.startsWith('+++ ')) {
410:       continue;
411:     }
412:     const raw = line.slice(4).trim();
413:     if (!raw || raw === '/dev/null') {
414:       continue;
415:     }
416:     const normalized = raw.replace(/^b\//, '').replace(/^\.\//, '').trim();
417:     if (!normalized || normalized === '/dev/null') {
418:       continue;
419:     }
420:     paths.add(normalized);
421:   }
422:   return Array.from(paths);
423: }
424: 
425: function parseDiffHunks(patch: string): Array<{ path: string; patch: string }> {
426:   const lines = patch.split('\n');
427:   const hunks: Array<{ path: string; patch: string }> = [];
428:   let currentPath: string | null = null;
429:   let currentLines: string[] = [];
430: 
431:   const flush = () => {
432:     if (!currentPath) {
433:       return;
434:     }
435:     hunks.push({
436:       path: currentPath,
437:       patch: currentLines.join('\n').trim(),
438:     });
439:   };
440: 
441:   for (const line of lines) {
442:     if (line.startsWith('+++ ')) {
443:       flush();
444:       const raw = line.slice(4).trim();
445:       currentPath = raw.replace(/^b\//, '').replace(/^\.\//, '').trim();
446:       currentLines = [line];
447:       continue;
448:     }
449:     if (currentPath) {
450:       currentLines.push(line);
451:     }
452:   }
453: 
454:   flush();
455:   return hunks.filter((hunk) => hunk.path && hunk.path !== '/dev/null');
456: }

458: function parentDirectories(path: string): string[] {
459:   const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean);
460:   const dirs = [''];
461:   let current = '';
462:   for (let index = 0; index < parts.length - 1; index += 1) {
463:     current = current ? `${current}/${parts[index]}` : parts[index] as string;
464:     dirs.push(current);
465:   }
466:   return dirs;
467: }

469: const CONVENTION_PATTERNS = [
470:   'AGENTS.md',
471:   'CODE_REVIEW.md',
472:   'CONTRIBUTING.md',
473:   '.editorconfig',
474:   '.eslintrc',
475:   '.eslintrc.js',
476:   '.eslintrc.cjs',
477:   '.eslintrc.json',
478:   '.eslintrc.yaml',
479:   '.eslintrc.yml',
480:   'eslint.config.js',
481:   'eslint.config.mjs',
482:   'eslint.config.cjs',
483:   'biome.json',
484:   'biome.jsonc',
485:   'prettier.config.js',
486:   'prettier.config.mjs',
487:   'prettier.config.cjs',
488:   '.prettierrc',
489:   '.prettierrc.json',
490:   '.prettierrc.yaml',
491:   '.prettierrc.yml',
492:   'pyproject.toml',
493:   'ruff.toml',
494:   'mypy.ini',
495:   'tsconfig.json',
496:   'tsconfig.base.json',
497:   'tsconfig.app.json',
498:   'tsconfig.node.json',
499: ] as const;

501: function discoverConventionCandidates(changedPaths: string[], maxCount = 10): string[] {
502:   const candidates = new Set<string>();
503:   for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
504:     const dirs = parentDirectories(changedPath);
505:     for (const dir of dirs) {
506:       for (const pattern of CONVENTION_PATTERNS) {
507:         const candidate = dir ? `${dir}/${pattern}` : pattern;
508:         candidates.add(candidate);
509:         if (candidates.size >= maxCount * 6) {
510:           return Array.from(candidates);
511:         }
512:       }
513:     }
514:   }
515:   return Array.from(candidates);
516: }

518: function estimateTokenCount(parts: string[]): number {
519:   const chars = parts.reduce((total, part) => total + part.length, 0);
520:   return Math.ceil(chars / 4);
521: }
522: 
523: function readOptionalString(value: unknown): string | null {
524:   return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() ? value.trim() : null;
525: }
526: 
527: function readOptionalNumber(value: unknown): number | null {
528:   return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value) ? value : null;
529: }
530: 
531: function stripSensitiveTokenFields(value: unknown): unknown {
532:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
533:     return value.map((item) => stripSensitiveTokenFields(item));
534:   }
535:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
536:     if (typeof value === 'string' && GITHUB_TOKEN_PATTERN_TEST.test(value)) {
537:       return value.replace(GITHUB_TOKEN_PATTERN, '[REDACTED_TOKEN]');
538:     }
539:     return value;
540:   }
541:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
542:   return Object.entries(record).reduce<Record<string, unknown>>((result, [key, nested]) => {
543:     const normalizedKey = key.toLowerCase();
544:     if (
545:       normalizedKey === 'x-review-github-token' ||
546:       normalizedKey === 'review_context_github_token' ||
547:       normalizedKey === 'authorization'
548:     ) {
549:       return result;
550:     }
551:     result[key] = stripSensitiveTokenFields(nested);
552:     return result;
553:   }, {});
554: }

556: function resolveReviewAnalysisModel(payload: Record<string, unknown>, env: Env): string {
557:   const requested = readOptionalString(payload.model);
558:   if (requested) {
559:     return requested;
560:   }
561:   const reviewModel = readOptionalString(env.REVIEW_MODEL);
562:   if (reviewModel) {
563:     return reviewModel;
564:   }
565:   const agentModel = readOptionalString(env.AGENT_MODEL);
566:   if (agentModel) {
567:     return agentModel;
568:   }
569:   return DEFAULT_REVIEW_MODEL;
570: }

572: function mergeProvenance(
573:   deploymentProvenance: Record<string, unknown>,
574:   reviewProvenance: Record<string, unknown>
575: ): Record<string, unknown> {
576:   const merged: Record<string, unknown> = {
577:     ...deploymentProvenance,
578:     ...reviewProvenance,
579:   };
580: 
581:   const deploymentSessionIds = parseStringArray(deploymentProvenance.sessionIds);
582:   const reviewSessionIds = parseStringArray(reviewProvenance.sessionIds);
583:   const mergedSessionIds = Array.from(new Set([...deploymentSessionIds, ...reviewSessionIds]));
584:   if (mergedSessionIds.length > 0) {
585:     merged.sessionIds = mergedSessionIds;
586:   }
587: 
588:   const deploymentIntent = parseStringArray(deploymentProvenance.intentSessionContext);
589:   const reviewIntent = parseStringArray(reviewProvenance.intentSessionContext);
590:   const mergedIntent = Array.from(new Set([...deploymentIntent, ...reviewIntent]));
591:   if (mergedIntent.length > 0) {
592:     merged.intentSessionContext = mergedIntent;
593:   }
594: 
595:   return merged;
596: }

598: function parseTouchedFilesFromMetadata(record: Record<string, unknown>): string[] {
599:   const candidates = [record.touchedFiles, record.touched_files, record.files_touched, record.changedFiles, record.changed_files, record.files];
600:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
601:     if (!Array.isArray(candidate)) {
602:       continue;
603:     }
604:     const parsed = candidate
605:       .flatMap((item) => {
606:         if (typeof item === 'string') {
607:           return [item.trim()];
608:         }
609:         const entry = asRecord(item);
610:         const path = readOptionalString(entry.path);
611:         return path ? [path] : [];
612:       })
613:       .filter(Boolean);
614:     if (parsed.length > 0) {
615:       return parsed;
616:     }
617:   }
618:   return [];
619: }

621: function parseLocalCochangeFromProvenance(value: unknown): {
622:   source: 'local_git';
623:   checkpointsRef: string;
624:   lookbackSessions: number;
625:   topN: number;
626:   sessionsScanned: number;
627:   relatedByChangedPath: Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>;
628: } | null {
629:   const record = asRecord(value);
630:   const source = readOptionalString(record.source);
631:   if (source !== 'local_git') {
632:     return null;
633:   }
634: 
635:   const checkpointsRef = readOptionalString(record.checkpointsRef) ?? 'entire/checkpoints/v1';
636:   const lookbackSessions = readOptionalNumber(record.lookbackSessions);
637:   const topN = readOptionalNumber(record.topN);
638:   const sessionsScanned = readOptionalNumber(record.sessionsScanned);
639:   const relatedByChangedPathRaw = asRecord(record.relatedByChangedPath);
640: 
641:   const relatedByChangedPath = Object.entries(relatedByChangedPathRaw).reduce<
642:     Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>
643:   >((acc, [changedPath, entries]) => {
644:     const key = changedPath.trim();
645:     if (!key || !Array.isArray(entries)) {
646:       return acc;
647:     }
648: 
649:     const normalized = entries
650:       .flatMap((entry) => {
651:         const item = asRecord(entry);
652:         const path = readOptionalString(item.path);
653:         const frequency = readOptionalNumber(item.frequency);
654:         const sessionIds = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(item.sessionIds));
655:         if (!path || frequency === null || frequency <= 0) {
656:           return [];
657:         }
658:         return [
659:           {
660:             path,
661:             frequency: Math.max(1, Math.floor(frequency)),
662:             sessionIds,
663:           },
664:         ];
665:       })
666:       .sort((left, right) => right.frequency - left.frequency)
667:       .slice(0, Math.max(1, Math.min(100, Math.floor(topN ?? 20))));
668: 
669:     acc[key] = normalized;
670:     return acc;
671:   }, {});
672: 
673:   return {
674:     source: 'local_git',
675:     checkpointsRef,
676:     lookbackSessions: Math.max(1, Math.min(50, Math.floor(lookbackSessions ?? 5))),
677:     topN: Math.max(1, Math.min(100, Math.floor(topN ?? 20))),
678:     sessionsScanned: Math.max(0, Math.floor(sessionsScanned ?? 0)),
679:     relatedByChangedPath,
680:   };
681: }

683: function buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken: string): Record<string, string> {
684:   const headers: Record<string, string> = {
685:     Accept: 'application/vnd.github+json',
686:     'X-GitHub-Api-Version': '2022-11-28',
687:     'User-Agent': 'nimbus-worker/1.0',
688:   };
689:   headers.Authorization = githubToken.toLowerCase().startsWith('bearer ') ? githubToken : `Bearer ${githubToken}`;
690:   return headers;
691: }
692: 
693: async function fetchGitHubJson(url: string, githubToken: string): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
694:   const response = await fetch(url, {
695:     headers: buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken),
696:   });
697: 
698:   if (!response.ok) {
699:     const rateLimited =
700:       response.status === 429 ||
701:       (response.status === 403 && (response.headers.get('x-ratelimit-remaining') ?? '').trim() === '0');
702:     const responseBody = redactReviewText(await response.text());
703:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
704:       'review_context_github_api_error',
705:       `GitHub API request failed (${response.status}) for ${url}${rateLimited ? ' [rate_limited]' : ''}`,
706:       responseBody
707:     );
708:   }
709: 
710:   const data = (await response.json()) as unknown;
711:   return asRecord(data);
712: }
713: 
714: async function fetchGitHubArray(url: string, githubToken: string): Promise<unknown[]> {
715:   const response = await fetch(url, {
716:     headers: buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken),
717:   });
718: 
719:   if (!response.ok) {
720:     const rateLimited =
721:       response.status === 429 ||
722:       (response.status === 403 && (response.headers.get('x-ratelimit-remaining') ?? '').trim() === '0');
723:     const responseBody = redactReviewText(await response.text());
724:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
725:       'review_context_github_api_error',
726:       `GitHub API request failed (${response.status}) for ${url}${rateLimited ? ' [rate_limited]' : ''}`,
727:       responseBody
728:     );
729:   }
730: 
731:   const data = (await response.json()) as unknown;
732:   return Array.isArray(data) ? data : [];
733: }
734: 
735: function classifyCochangeSkipReason(error: unknown): 'rate_limited' | 'github_api_error' | 'cache_error' {
736:   if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError && error.code === 'review_context_cache_error') {
737:     return 'cache_error';
738:   }
739:   if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError && /\[rate_limited\]/.test(error.message)) {
740:     return 'rate_limited';
741:   }
742:   if (isCochangeCacheError(error)) {
743:     return 'cache_error';
744:   }
745:   return 'github_api_error';
746: }
747: 
748: async function fetchCochangeFromCheckpointBranch(
749:   repo: string,
750:   changedPaths: string[],
751:   lookbackSessions: number,
752:   githubToken: string
753: ): Promise<{
754:   relatedByChangedPath: Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>;
755:   sessionsScanned: number;
756: }> {
757:   const commits = await fetchGitHubArray(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/commits?sha=entire/checkpoints/v1&per_page=${lookbackSessions}`, githubToken);
758: 
759:   const frequencyByChangedPath = new Map<string, Map<string, { count: number; sessions: Set<string> }>>();
760:   for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
761:     frequencyByChangedPath.set(changedPath, new Map());
762:   }
763:   let sessionsScanned = 0;
764: 
765:   for (const commit of commits.slice(0, lookbackSessions)) {
766:     const commitRecord = asRecord(commit);
767:     const sha = readOptionalString(commitRecord.sha);
768:     if (!sha) {
769:       continue;
770:     }
771: 
772:     const detail = await fetchGitHubJson(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/commits/${sha}`, githubToken);
773:     const files = Array.isArray(detail.files) ? detail.files : [];
774:     const metadataPaths = files
775:       .map((entry) => readOptionalString(asRecord(entry).filename))
776:       .filter((path): path is string => Boolean(path && path.endsWith('/metadata.json')))
777:       .slice(0, 3);
778: 
779:     const touchedFiles = new Set<string>();
780:     for (const metadataPath of metadataPaths) {
781:       const file = await fetchGitHubJson(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/contents/${metadataPath}?ref=${sha}`, githubToken);
782:       const content = readOptionalString(file.content);
783:       if (!content) {
784:         continue;
785:       }
786:       let decoded = '';
787:       try {
788:         decoded = atob(content.replace(/\n/g, ''));
789:       } catch {
790:         continue;
791:       }
792:       try {
793:         const metadata = JSON.parse(decoded) as unknown;
794:         for (const path of parseTouchedFilesFromMetadata(asRecord(metadata))) {
795:           touchedFiles.add(path);
796:         }
797:       } catch {
798:         continue;
799:       }
800:     }
801: 
802:     if (touchedFiles.size === 0) {
803:       continue;
804:     }
805: 
806:     sessionsScanned += 1;
807:     for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
808:       if (!touchedFiles.has(changedPath)) {
809:         continue;
810:       }
811:       const frequency = frequencyByChangedPath.get(changedPath);
812:       if (!frequency) {
813:         continue;
814:       }
815:       for (const path of touchedFiles) {
816:         if (path === changedPath) {
817:           continue;
818:         }
819:         const next = frequency.get(path) ?? { count: 0, sessions: new Set<string>() };
820:         next.count += 1;
821:         next.sessions.add(sha);
822:         frequency.set(path, next);
823:       }
824:     }
825:   }
826: 
827:   const relatedByChangedPath: Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>> = {};
828:   for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
829:     const frequency =
830:       frequencyByChangedPath.get(changedPath) ?? new Map<string, { count: number; sessions: Set<string> }>();
831:     relatedByChangedPath[changedPath] = Array.from(frequency.entries())
832:       .map(([path, value]) => ({
833:         path,
834:         frequency: value.count,
835:         sessionIds: Array.from(value.sessions),
836:       }))
837:       .sort((left, right) => right.frequency - left.frequency);
838:   }
839: 
840:   return {
841:     relatedByChangedPath,
842:     sessionsScanned,
843:   };
844: }
845: 
846: async function assembleReviewContextBootstrap(
847:   env: Env,
848:   review: ReviewRunResponse,
849:   reviewPayload: Record<string, unknown>,
850:   options?: ReviewRunExecutionOptions
851: ): Promise<ReviewContext> {
852:   const COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS = 5;
853:   const COCHANGE_TOP_N = 20;
854:   const CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT = 10;
855: 
856:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
857:     reviewId: review.id,
858:     eventType: 'review_context_assembly_started',
859:     payload: {
860:       source: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
861:     },
862:   });
863: 
864:   const workspace = await getWorkspace(env.DB, review.workspaceId);
865:   const checkpointId = workspace?.checkpointId?.trim() ?? '';
866:   if (!checkpointId) {
867:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
868:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
869:       'Review context assembly requires an Entire checkpoint-backed workspace with a checkpointId.'
870:     );
871:   }
872: 
873:   const deployment = await getWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, review.workspaceId, review.deploymentId);
874:   if (!deployment) {
875:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError('review_context_deployment_not_found', `Deployment ${review.deploymentId} was not found.`);
876:   }
877: 
878:   const deploymentRequest = (await getWorkspaceDeploymentRequestPayload(env.DB, review.deploymentId)) ?? {};
879:   const deploymentRequestProvenance = asRecord(deploymentRequest.provenance);
880:   const reviewRequestProvenance = asRecord(reviewPayload.provenance);
881:   const requestProvenance = mergeProvenance(deploymentRequestProvenance, reviewRequestProvenance);
882:   const sessionIds = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(requestProvenance.sessionIds));
883:   if (sessionIds.length === 0) {
884:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
885:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
886:       'Review context assembly requires at least one Entire sessionId in deployment provenance.'
887:     );
888:   }
889: 
890:   const sessionId = sessionIds[0] ?? '';
891:   const sessionIntentCandidates = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(requestProvenance.intentSessionContext));
892:   const sessionIntent = sessionIntentCandidates[0] ?? null;
893:   const attributionTrailer = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.attributionTrailer);
894:   const agentType = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.agentType);
895:   const requestedTokenBudget =
896:     readOptionalNumber(requestProvenance.reviewContextTokenBudget) ??
897:     readOptionalNumber(requestProvenance.contextTokenBudget) ??
898:     null;
899:   const configuredTokenBudget = readOptionalNumber(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET);
900:   const tokenBudget = requestedTokenBudget ?? configuredTokenBudget ?? null;
901: 
902:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
903:     reviewId: review.id,
904:     eventType: 'review_context_checkpoint_context_collected',
905:     payload: {
906:       checkpointId,
907:       sessionId,
908:       sessionCount: sessionIds.length,
909:       hasSessionIntent: Boolean(sessionIntent),
910:     },
911:   });
912: 
913:   const result = asRecord(deployment.result);
914:   const resultProvenance = asRecord(result.provenance);
915:   const resultArtifact = asRecord(result.artifact);
916:   const provenanceOperationId = typeof resultProvenance.operationId === 'string'
917:     ? resultProvenance.operationId
918:     : typeof requestProvenance.operationId === 'string'
919:       ? requestProvenance.operationId
920:       : null;
921:   const reviewDiffArtifactId = typeof resultArtifact.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
922:     ? resultArtifact.reviewDiffArtifactId
923:     : typeof resultProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
924:       ? resultProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId
925:       : typeof requestProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
926:         ? requestProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId
927:         : null;
928: 
929:   const authoritativeDiff = await loadAuthoritativeDeploymentDiff(
930:     env,
931:     review.workspaceId,
932:     provenanceOperationId,
933:     reviewDiffArtifactId
934:   );
935:   const commitDiffPatch = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.commitDiffPatch);
936:   const authoritativeDiffPatch = readOptionalString(authoritativeDiff?.patch);
937:   const diffPatch = authoritativeDiffPatch ?? commitDiffPatch ?? null;
938:   if (!diffPatch) {
939:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
940:       'review_context_diff_missing',
941:       'Review context assembly requires non-empty diff patch context. Ensure deployment provenance includes review diff artifact or commit diff patch.'
942:     );
943:   }
944:   const changedPaths = diffPatch ? parseChangedPathsFromDiff(diffPatch) : [];
945:   const diffHunks = diffPatch ? parseDiffHunks(diffPatch) : [];
946: 
947:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
948:     reviewId: review.id,
949:     eventType: 'review_context_diff_collected',
950:     payload: {
951:       source: authoritativeDiffPatch ? authoritativeDiff?.source ?? null : commitDiffPatch ? 'commit_patch' : null,
952:       artifactId: authoritativeDiffPatch ? authoritativeDiff?.artifactId ?? null : null,
953:       hasDiff: Boolean(diffPatch),
954:       patchBytes: diffPatch ? new TextEncoder().encode(diffPatch).byteLength : 0,
955:       fallbackUsed: !authoritativeDiffPatch && Boolean(commitDiffPatch),
956:     },
957:   });
958: 
959:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
960:     reviewId: review.id,
961:     eventType: 'review_context_changed_files_collected',
962:     payload: {
963:       changedFileCount: changedPaths.length,
964:     },
965:   });
966: 
967:   const deploymentSourceBundleKey =
968:     typeof resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey === 'string' && resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey.trim()
969:       ? resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey.trim()
970:       : deployment.sourceBundleKey ?? null;
971:   if (!deploymentSourceBundleKey) {
972:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
973:       'review_context_source_bundle_missing',
974:       'Review context assembly requires deployment source bundle key.'
975:     );
976:   }
977: 
978:   const changedFileReads = changedPaths.length
979:     ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
980:         sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
981:         sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-changed`,
982:         paths: changedPaths,
983:       })
984:     : [];
985:   const changedFiles = changedFileReads
986:     .filter((item) => item.content !== null && !item.error)
987:     .map((item) => ({
988:       path: item.path,
989:       content: item.content ?? '',
990:       byteSize: item.bytes,
991:       source: 'changed' as const,
992:     }));
993: 
994:   const conventionCandidates = discoverConventionCandidates(changedPaths, CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT);
995:   const conventionReads = conventionCandidates.length
996:     ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
997:         sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
998:         sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-conventions`,
999:         paths: conventionCandidates,
1000:       })
1001:     : [];
1002:   const conventionFiles = conventionReads
1003:     .filter((item) => item.content !== null && !item.error)
1004:     .slice(0, CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT)
1005:     .map((item) => ({
1006:       path: item.path,
1007:       content: item.content ?? '',
1008:       byteSize: item.bytes,
1009:       source: 'convention' as const,
1010:     }));
1011: 
1012:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1013:     reviewId: review.id,
1014:     eventType: 'review_context_conventions_collected',
1015:     payload: {
1016:       candidateCount: conventionCandidates.length,
1017:       conventionFileCount: conventionFiles.length,
1018:       maxCount: CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT,
1019:     },
1020:   });
1021: 
1022:   const repoSlug =
1023:     readOptionalString(requestProvenance.repo) ??
1024:     readOptionalString(requestProvenance.repository) ??
1025:     readOptionalString(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_REPO);
1026:   if (!repoSlug) {
1027:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1028:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
1029:       'Review context assembly requires repository slug in deployment provenance (provenance.repo).'
1030:     );
1031:   }
1032:   let relatedFiles: Array<{
1033:     path: string;
1034:     content: string;
1035:     byteSize: number;
1036:     source: 'related';
1037:     score: number;
1038:     coChangeFrequency: number;
1039:     supportingSessionIds: string[];
1040:   }> = [];
1041:   let sessionsScanned = 0;
1042:   let coChangeSkipped = false;
1043:   let coChangeSkipReason: string | null = null;
1044:   let coChangeAvailable = false;
1045:   let coChangeSource: 'entire/checkpoints/v1' | 'local_git' = 'entire/checkpoints/v1';
1046:   let coChangeLookbackSessions = COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS;
1047:   let coChangeTopN = COCHANGE_TOP_N;
1048:   const localCochange = parseLocalCochangeFromProvenance(requestProvenance.localCochange);
1049:   const githubToken = readOptionalString(options?.cochangeGithubToken) ?? readOptionalString(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_GITHUB_TOKEN);
1050: 
1051:   try {
1052:     const effectiveLookback = localCochange?.lookbackSessions ?? COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS;
1053:     const effectiveTopN = localCochange?.topN ?? COCHANGE_TOP_N;
1054:     coChangeLookbackSessions = effectiveLookback;
1055:     coChangeTopN = effectiveTopN;
1056:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1057:       reviewId: review.id,
1058:       eventType: 'review_context_cochange_lookup_started',
1059:       payload: {
1060:         repo: repoSlug,
1061:         lookbackSessions: effectiveLookback,
1062:         source: localCochange ? 'local_git' : 'github_api',
1063:       },
1064:     });
1065: 
1066:     const relatedFrequency = new Map<string, { frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>();
1067:     const entriesByChangedPath = new Map<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>();
1068: 
1069:     if (localCochange) {
1070:       coChangeSource = 'local_git';
1071:       sessionsScanned = localCochange.sessionsScanned;
1072:       for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
1073:         entriesByChangedPath.set(changedPath, localCochange.relatedByChangedPath[changedPath] ?? []);
1074:       }
1075:       if (changedPaths.length > 0) {
1076:         const cacheUpserts = changedPaths.map((changedPath) => ({
1077:           filePath: changedPath,
1078:           repo: repoSlug,
1079:           branch: localCochange.checkpointsRef,
1080:           cochange: entriesByChangedPath.get(changedPath) ?? [],
1081:           lookbackSessions: localCochange.lookbackSessions,
1082:         }));
1083:         await upsertReviewCochangeCacheBatch(env.DB, cacheUpserts);
1084:       }
1085:     } else {
1086:       if (!githubToken) {
1087:         throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1088:           'review_context_github_token_missing',
1089:           'co-change retrieval requires a GitHub token - set REVIEW_CONTEXT_GITHUB_TOKEN in your local .env'
1090:         );
1091:       }
1092: 
1093:       const changedPathsMissingCache: string[] = [];
1094:       const cachedRows = await getReviewCochangeCacheBatch(env.DB, {
1095:         repo: repoSlug,
1096:         filePaths: changedPaths,
1097:       });
1098:       const cacheByPath = new Map(cachedRows.map((row) => [row.filePath, row]));
1099: 
1100:       for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
1101:         const cached = cacheByPath.get(changedPath);
1102:         const entries = cached?.cochange;
1103:         const cachedLookbackSessions = cached?.lookbackSessions ?? null;
1104:         if (entries && cachedLookbackSessions === COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS) {
1105:           entriesByChangedPath.set(changedPath, entries);
1106:         } else {
1107:           changedPathsMissingCache.push(changedPath);
1108:         }
1109:       }
1110: 
1111:       if (changedPathsMissingCache.length > 0) {
1112:         const fetched = await fetchCochangeFromCheckpointBranch(repoSlug, changedPathsMissingCache, COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS, githubToken);
1113:         sessionsScanned += fetched.sessionsScanned;
1114:         const cacheUpserts: Array<{
1115:           filePath: string;
1116:           repo: string;
1117:           branch: string;
1118:           cochange: Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>;
1119:           lookbackSessions: number;
1120:         }> = [];
1121:         for (const changedPath of changedPathsMissingCache) {
1122:           const entries = fetched.relatedByChangedPath[changedPath] ?? [];
1123:           entriesByChangedPath.set(changedPath, entries);
1124:           cacheUpserts.push({
1125:             filePath: changedPath,
1126:             repo: repoSlug,
1127:             branch: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
1128:             cochange: entries,
1129:             lookbackSessions: COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1130:           });
1131:         }
1132:         await upsertReviewCochangeCacheBatch(env.DB, cacheUpserts);
1133:       }
1134:     }
1135: 
1136:     for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
1137:       const entries = entriesByChangedPath.get(changedPath) ?? [];
1138:       for (const entry of entries) {
1139:         const existing = relatedFrequency.get(entry.path) ?? { frequency: 0, sessionIds: [] };
1140:         existing.frequency += entry.frequency;
1141:         existing.sessionIds = Array.from(new Set([...existing.sessionIds, ...entry.sessionIds]));
1142:         relatedFrequency.set(entry.path, existing);
1143:       }
1144:     }
1145: 
1146:     const changedPathSet = new Set(changedPaths);
1147:     const rankedRelated = Array.from(relatedFrequency.entries())
1148:       .map(([path, value]) => ({ path, ...value }))
1149:       .filter((item) => !changedPathSet.has(item.path))
1150:       .sort((left, right) => right.frequency - left.frequency)
1151:       .slice(0, effectiveTopN);
1152: 
1153:     const relatedReads = rankedRelated.length
1154:       ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
1155:           sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
1156:           sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-related`,
1157:           paths: rankedRelated.map((item) => item.path),
1158:         })
1159:       : [];
1160:     const readByPath = new Map(relatedReads.map((item) => [item.path, item]));
1161:     relatedFiles = rankedRelated
1162:       .flatMap((item) => {
1163:         const read = readByPath.get(item.path);
1164:         if (!read || read.content === null || read.error) {
1165:           return [];
1166:         }
1167:         return [
1168:           {
1169:             path: item.path,
1170:             content: read.content,
1171:             byteSize: read.bytes,
1172:             source: 'related' as const,
1173:             score: item.frequency,
1174:             coChangeFrequency: item.frequency,
1175:             supportingSessionIds: item.sessionIds,
1176:           },
1177:         ];
1178:       });
1179:     coChangeAvailable = relatedFiles.length > 0;
1180: 
1181:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1182:       reviewId: review.id,
1183:       eventType: 'review_context_cochange_lookup_completed',
1184:       payload: {
1185:         repo: repoSlug,
1186:         relatedFileCount: relatedFiles.length,
1187:         topN: effectiveTopN,
1188:         source: localCochange ? 'local_git' : 'github_api',
1189:       },
1190:     });
1191:   } catch (error) {
1192:     const reason = classifyCochangeSkipReason(error);
1193:     const sanitizedErrorDetails = redactReviewText(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
1194:     const cacheErrorDetails = isCochangeCacheError(error) ? sanitizedErrorDetails : null;
1195:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1196:       reviewId: review.id,
1197:       eventType: 'review_context_cochange_failed',
1198:       payload: {
1199:         reason,
1200:         repo: repoSlug,
1201:         lookbackSessions: localCochange?.lookbackSessions ?? COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1202:         source: localCochange ? 'local_git' : 'github_api',
1203:         githubResponseBody: error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError ? error.details : sanitizedErrorDetails,
1204:       },
1205:     });
1206:     if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError) {
1207:       throw error;
1208:     }
1209:     if (reason === 'cache_error') {
1210:       throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1211:         'review_context_cache_error',
1212:         'Co-change context cache read/write failed (cache_error).',
1213:         cacheErrorDetails
1214:       );
1215:     }
1216:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1217:       'review_context_github_api_error',
1218:       `Co-change context retrieval failed (${reason}).`
1219:     );
1220:   }
1221: 
1222:   const assembledAt = new Date().toISOString();
1223:   const contextId = generateReviewContextId();
1224:   const contextPayload: ReviewContext = {
1225:     id: contextId,
1226:     reviewId: review.id,
1227:     workspaceId: review.workspaceId,
1228:     deploymentId: review.deploymentId,
1229:     commitSha: workspace?.commitSha ?? '',
1230:     assembledAt,
1231:     checkpoint: {
1232:       checkpointId,
1233:       branch: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
1234:       attributionTrailer,
1235:       session: {
1236:         sessionId,
1237:         agentType,
1238:         sessionIntent,
1239:       },
1240:     },
1241:     retrieval: {
1242:       changedFiles,
1243:       diffHunks,
1244:       relatedFiles,
1245:       conventionFiles,
1246:       coChange: {
1247:         source: coChangeSource,
1248:         lookbackSessions: coChangeLookbackSessions,
1249:         sessionsScanned,
1250:         filesConsidered: changedPaths.length,
1251:         topN: coChangeTopN,
1252:         coChangeSkipped,
1253:         coChangeSkipReason,
1254:         coChangeAvailable,
1255:       },
1256:     },
1257:     stats: {
1258:       totalFilesIncluded: changedFiles.length + relatedFiles.length + conventionFiles.length,
1259:       totalBytesIncluded:
1260:         changedFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0) +
1261:         relatedFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0) +
1262:         conventionFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0),
1263:       estimatedTokens: estimateTokenCount([
1264:         diffPatch ?? '',
1265:         ...changedFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1266:         ...relatedFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1267:         ...conventionFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1268:       ]),
1269:       tokenBudget,
1270:     },
1271:   };
1272: 
1273:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1274:     reviewId: review.id,
1275:     eventType: 'review_context_budget_checked',
1276:     payload: {
1277:       estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1278:       tokenBudget,
1279:       tokenBudgetSource:
1280:         requestedTokenBudget !== null
1281:           ? 'request_provenance'
1282:           : configuredTokenBudget !== null
1283:             ? 'configured_env'
1284:             : 'unconfigured',
1285:       exceeded: tokenBudget !== null && contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens > tokenBudget,
1286:     },
1287:   });
1288: 
1289:   if (tokenBudget !== null && contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens > tokenBudget) {
1290:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1291:       'review_context_budget_exceeded',
1292:       `ReviewContext estimated token usage (${contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens}) exceeds configured budget (${tokenBudget}). Increase the budget and retry.`
1293:     );
1294:   }
1295: 
1296:   const storageBucketCandidates = [env.REVIEW_CONTEXTS, env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS, env.SOURCE_BUNDLES];
1297:   const storageBucket = storageBucketCandidates.find(
1298:     (bucket): bucket is R2Bucket => Boolean(bucket && typeof bucket.put === 'function')
1299:   );
1300:   if (!storageBucket) {
1301:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1302:       'review_context_storage_unavailable',
1303:       'REVIEW_CONTEXTS, WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS, or SOURCE_BUNDLES R2 binding is required for review context assembly.'
1304:     );
1305:   }
1306: 
1307:   const r2Key = `review-context/${review.id}/${contextId}.json`;
1308:   const serialized = JSON.stringify(stripSensitiveTokenFields(contextPayload));
1309:   await storageBucket.put(r2Key, serialized, {
1310:     httpMetadata: {
1311:       contentType: 'application/json',
1312:     },
1313:   });
1314:   const ref = await createReviewContextBlobReference(env.DB, {
1315:     id: contextId,
1316:     reviewId: review.id,
1317:     workspaceId: review.workspaceId,
1318:     deploymentId: review.deploymentId,
1319:     r2Key,
1320:     byteSize: new TextEncoder().encode(serialized).byteLength,
1321:     estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1322:   });
1323: 
1324:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1325:     reviewId: review.id,
1326:     eventType: 'review_context_stored',
1327:     payload: {
1328:       contextId: ref.id,
1329:       r2Key: ref.r2Key,
1330:       totalFilesIncluded: contextPayload.stats.totalFilesIncluded,
1331:       totalBytesIncluded: contextPayload.stats.totalBytesIncluded,
1332:       estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1333:     },
1334:   });
1335: 
1336:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1337:     reviewId: review.id,
1338:     eventType: 'review_context_assembly_succeeded',
1339:     payload: {
1340:       checkpointId,
1341:       sessionId,
1342:       changedFileCount: changedPaths.length,
1343:       contextId: ref.id,
1344:     },
1345:   });
1346: 
1347:   return contextPayload;
1348: }
1349: 
1350: async function loadAuthoritativeDeploymentDiff(
1351:   env: Env,
1352:   workspaceId: string,
1353:   operationId: string | null,
1354:   reviewDiffArtifactId: string | null
1355: ): Promise<{ source: 'artifact_patch'; artifactId: string; patch: string } | null> {
1356:   const getArtifactObject = async (objectKey: string): Promise<R2ObjectBody | null> => {
1357:     const fromArtifacts = env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS ? await env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS.get(objectKey) : null;
1358:     if (fromArtifacts) {
1359:       return fromArtifacts;
1360:     }
1361:     return env.SOURCE_BUNDLES ? await env.SOURCE_BUNDLES.get(objectKey) : null;
1362:   };
1363: 
1364:   if (reviewDiffArtifactId) {
1365:     const reviewArtifact = await getWorkspaceArtifactById(env.DB, workspaceId, reviewDiffArtifactId);
1366:     if (reviewArtifact && reviewArtifact.artifact.type === 'patch') {
1367:       const object = await getArtifactObject(reviewArtifact.objectKey);
1368:       if (!object) {
1369:         return null;
1370:       }
1371:       return {
1372:         source: 'artifact_patch',
1373:         artifactId: reviewDiffArtifactId,
1374:         patch: await object.text(),
1375:       };
1376:     }
1377:   }
1378: 
1379:   if (!operationId) {
1380:     return null;
1381:   }
1382: 
1383:   const operation = await getWorkspaceOperation(env.DB, workspaceId, operationId);
1384:   if (!operation || operation.type !== 'export_patch' || operation.status !== 'succeeded') {
1385:     return null;
1386:   }
1387: 
1388:   const result = asRecord(operation.result);
1389:   const artifactId = typeof result.artifactId === 'string' ? result.artifactId.trim() : '';
1390:   if (!artifactId) {
1391:     return null;
1392:   }
1393: 
1394:   const artifact = await getWorkspaceArtifactById(env.DB, workspaceId, artifactId);
1395:   if (!artifact || artifact.artifact.type !== 'patch') {
1396:     return null;
1397:   }
1398: 
1399:   const object = await getArtifactObject(artifact.objectKey);
1400:   if (!object) {
1401:     return null;
1402:   }
1403: 
1404:   return {
1405:     source: 'artifact_patch',
1406:     artifactId,
1407:     patch: await object.text(),
1408:   };
1409: }
1410: 
1411: async function buildWorkspaceDeploymentReport(
1412:   env: Env,
1413:   review: ReviewRunResponse,
1414:   payload: Record<string, unknown>,
1415:   reviewContext: ReviewContext
1416: ): Promise<ReviewReport> {
1417:   const deployment = await getWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, review.workspaceId, review.deploymentId);
1418:   if (!deployment) {
1419:     throw new Error(`Deployment not found for review target ${review.deploymentId}`);
1420:   }
1421: 
1422:   const deploymentRequest = (await getWorkspaceDeploymentRequestPayload(env.DB, review.deploymentId)) ?? {};
1423:   const deploymentEvents = await listWorkspaceDeploymentEvents(env.DB, review.workspaceId, review.deploymentId, 0, 500);
1424:   const reviewPolicy = asRecord(payload.policy);
1425:   const reviewFormat = asRecord(payload.format);
1426:   const reviewAnalysisModel = resolveReviewAnalysisModel(payload, env);
1427:   const result = asRecord(deployment.result);
1428:   const resultProvenance = asRecord(result.provenance);
1429:   const resultArtifact = asRecord(result.artifact);
1430:   const requestValidation = asRecord(deploymentRequest.validation);
1431:   const requestProvenance = mergeProvenance(asRecord(deploymentRequest.provenance), asRecord(payload.provenance));
1432:   const intentSessionContext = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(requestProvenance.intentSessionContext)).slice(0, 8);
1433:   const provenanceTaskId = typeof resultProvenance.taskId === 'string'
1434:     ? resultProvenance.taskId
1435:     : typeof requestProvenance.taskId === 'string'
1436:       ? requestProvenance.taskId
1437:       : null;
1438:   const provenanceTask = provenanceTaskId ? await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, review.workspaceId, provenanceTaskId) : null;
1439:   const taskResult = asRecord(provenanceTask?.result);
1440:   const severityThreshold = typeof reviewPolicy.severityThreshold === 'string' ? reviewPolicy.severityThreshold : 'low';
1441:   const maxFindings = parsePositiveInteger(reviewPolicy.maxFindings, 100, 500);
1442:   const includeProvenance = parseBoolean(reviewPolicy.includeProvenance, true);
1443:   const includeValidationEvidence = parseBoolean(reviewPolicy.includeValidationEvidence, true);
1444:   const includeMarkdownSummary = parseBoolean(reviewFormat.includeMarkdownSummary, true);
1445:   const changedFileCount = reviewContext.retrieval.coChange.filesConsidered;
1446:   const advisories =
1447:     changedFileCount > LARGE_DIFF_ADVISORY_THRESHOLD
1448:       ? [
1449:           `Large diff detected (${changedFileCount} files). Consider smaller, focused commits for higher quality reviews.`,
1450:         ]
1451:       : [];
1452: 
1453:   const baseGoal =
1454:     typeof provenanceTask?.prompt === 'string' && provenanceTask.prompt.trim()
1455:       ? provenanceTask.prompt.trim()
1456:       : typeof requestProvenance.note === 'string' && requestProvenance.note.trim()
1457:         ? requestProvenance.note.trim()
1458:       : `Assess workspace deployment ${review.deploymentId} for review-first handoff readiness.`;

1773:       reviewId: review.id,
1774:       eventType: 'review_finding_emitted',
1775:       payload: {
1776:         severity: finding.severity,
1777:         category: finding.category,
1778:         passType: finding.passType,
1779:         description: finding.description,
1780:       },
1781:     });
1782:   }
1783: 
1784:   return report;
1785: }
1786: 
1787: export async function processReviewRun(env: Env, reviewId: string, options?: ReviewRunExecutionOptions): Promise<void> {
1788:   const claimed = await claimReviewRunForExecution(env.DB, reviewId);
1789:   if (!claimed) {
1790:     const existing = await getReviewRun(env.DB, reviewId);
1791:     if (existing?.status === 'running') {
1792:       const attemptTimeoutMs = parseTimeoutMs(env.ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_MS, DEFAULT_REVIEW_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_MS);
1793:       const staleThresholdMs = attemptTimeoutMs + REVIEW_STALE_GRACE_MS;
1794:       const startedAtMs = toTimestampMs(existing.startedAt) ?? toTimestampMs(existing.updatedAt) ?? toTimestampMs(existing.createdAt);
1795:       const staleForMs = startedAtMs === null ? null : Date.now() - startedAtMs;
1796:       const isStale = typeof staleForMs === 'number' && staleForMs >= staleThresholdMs;
1797:       if (isStale) {
1798:         const staleForSeconds = Math.floor(staleForMs / 1000);
1799:         const message = `Review execution stalled in running state for ${staleForSeconds}s (timeout threshold ${Math.floor(staleThresholdMs / 1000)}s).`;
1800:         const attemptCount = existing.attemptCount ?? 0;
1801:         if ((options?.allowRetryScheduling ?? true) && attemptCount <= REVIEW_MAX_RETRIES) {
1802:           await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, []);
1803:           await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'queued', {
1804:             report: null,
1805:             markdownSummary: null,
1806:             startedAt: null,
1807:             finishedAt: null,
1808:             errorCode: 'retry_scheduled',
1809:             errorMessage: message,
1810:           });
1811:           await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1812:             reviewId,
1813:             eventType: 'review_retry_scheduled',
1814:             payload: {
1815:               attemptCount,
1816:               maxRetries: REVIEW_MAX_RETRIES,
1817:               reason: 'stale_running_timeout',
1818:               staleForSeconds,
1819:             },
1820:           });
1821:           throw new QueueRetryError('Review stale-running recovery requested');
1822:         }
1823: 
1824:         await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, []);
1825:         await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'failed', {
1826:           report: null,
1827:           markdownSummary: null,
1828:           errorCode: 'review_execution_timeout',
1829:           errorMessage: message,
1830:         });
1831:         await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1832:           reviewId,
1833:           eventType: 'review_failed',
1834:           payload: {
1835:             code: 'review_execution_timeout',
1836:             message,
1837:           },
1838:         });
1839:         return;
1840:       }
1841:       throw new QueueRetryError('Review run is already running; defer redelivery');
1842:     }
1843:     return;
1844:   }
1845: 
1846:   let review: ReviewRunResponse | null = null;
1847:   try {
1848:     review = await getReviewRun(env.DB, reviewId);
1849:     if (!review) {
1850:       return;
1851:     }
1852: 
1853:     const payload = await getReviewRunRequestPayload(env.DB, reviewId);
1854:     if (!payload) {
1855:       await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'failed', {
1856:         errorCode: 'review_not_found',
1857:         errorMessage: 'Review request payload no longer exists',
1858:       });
1859:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1860:         reviewId,
1861:         eventType: 'review_failed',
1862:         payload: {
1863:           code: 'review_not_found',
1864:           message: 'Review request payload no longer exists',
1865:         },
1866:       });
1867:       return;
1868:     }
1869: 
1870:     const reviewContext = await assembleReviewContextBootstrap(env, review, payload, options);
1871:     const report = await executeReviewRun(env, review, payload, reviewContext);
1872:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1873:       reviewId,
1874:       eventType: 'review_finalize_started',
1875:       payload: {
1876:         findingCount: report.findings.length,
1877:       },
1878:     });
1879:     await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, report.findings);
1880:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1881:       reviewId,
1882:       eventType: 'review_analysis_findings_persisted',
1883:       payload: {
1884:         findingCount: report.findings.length,
1885:       },
1886:     });
1887:     await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'succeeded', {
1888:       report,
1889:       markdownSummary: report.markdownSummary,
1890:       errorCode: null,
1891:       errorMessage: null,
1892:     });
1893:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1894:       reviewId,
1895:       eventType: 'review_analysis_succeeded',
1896:       payload: {
1897:         findingCount: report.findings.length,
1898:       },
1899:     });
1900:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1901:       reviewId,
1902:       eventType: 'review_succeeded',
1903:       payload: {
1904:         recommendation: report.summary.recommendation,
1905:         findingCount: report.findings.length,
1906:       },
1907:     });
1908:   } catch (error) {
1909:     const message = formatReviewAnalysisError(error);
1910:     const latest = await getReviewRun(env.DB, reviewId);
1911:     const attemptCount = latest?.attemptCount ?? review?.attemptCount ?? 0;
1912: 
1913:     const allowRetryScheduling = options?.allowRetryScheduling !== false;
1914:     if (allowRetryScheduling && (error instanceof QueueRetryError || transientReviewFailure(message)) && attemptCount <= REVIEW_MAX_RETRIES) {
1915:       await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, []);
1916:       await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'queued', {
1917:         report: null,
1918:         markdownSummary: null,
1919:         startedAt: null,
1920:         finishedAt: null,
1921:         errorCode: 'retry_scheduled',
1922:         errorMessage: message,
1923:       });
1924:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1925:         reviewId,
1926:         eventType: 'review_retry_scheduled',
1927:         payload: {
1928:           attemptCount,
1929:           maxRetries: REVIEW_MAX_RETRIES,
1930:         },
1931:       });
1932:       throw new QueueRetryError('Review transient failure; retry requested');
1933:     }
1934: 
1935:     const contextAssemblyErrorCode = error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError ? error.code : null;
1936:     const finalErrorCode = contextAssemblyErrorCode ?? 'review_execution_failed';
1937:     await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'failed', {
1938:       errorCode: finalErrorCode,
1939:       errorMessage: message,
1940:     });
1941:     try {
1942:       if (contextAssemblyErrorCode) {
1943:         await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1944:           reviewId,
1945:           eventType: 'review_context_assembly_failed',
1946:           payload: {
1947:             code: contextAssemblyErrorCode,
1948:             message,
1949:           },
1950:         });
1951:       }
1952:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1953:         reviewId,
1954:         eventType: 'review_failed',
1955:         payload: {
1956:           code: finalErrorCode,
1957:           message,
1958:         },
1959:       });
1960:     } catch {
1961:       // Best-effort terminal event.
1962:     }
1963:   }
1964: }
1965: 
1966: export async function runReviewInlineWithRetries(
1967:   env: Env,
1968:   reviewId: string,
1969:   maxCycles = 4,
1970:   options?: ReviewRunExecutionOptions
1971: ): Promise<void> {
1972:   for (let cycle = 0; cycle < maxCycles; cycle += 1) {
1973:     try {
1974:       await processReviewRun(env, reviewId, options);
1975:     } catch {
1976:       // Retry scheduling is inferred from persisted status.
1977:     }
1978: 
1979:     const latest = await getReviewRun(env.DB, reviewId);
1980:     if (!latest) {
1981:       return;
1982:     }
1983:     if (latest.status !== 'queued') {
1984:       return;
1985:     }
1986:     if (latest.error?.code !== 'retry_scheduled') {
1987:       return;
1988:     }
1989:   }
1990: 
1991:   const latest = await getReviewRun(env.DB, reviewId);
1992:   if (latest?.status === 'queued' && latest.error?.code === 'retry_scheduled') {
1993:     const message = `Review ${reviewId} remained queued after inline retries`;
1994:     await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, []);
1995:     await updateReviewRunStatus(env.DB, reviewId, 'failed', {
1996:       report: null,
1997:       markdownSummary: null,
1998:       errorCode: 'review_execution_failed',
1999:       errorMessage: message,
2000:     });
2001:     await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
2002:       reviewId,
2003:       eventType: 'review_failed',
2004:       payload: {
2005:         code: 'review_execution_failed',
2006:         message,
2007:       },
2008:     });
2009:   }
2010: }
2011: 
2012: export function shouldRetryReviewError(error: unknown): boolean {
2013:   if (error instanceof QueueRetryError) {
2014:     return true;
2015:   }
2016: 
2017:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
2018:   return transientReviewFailure(message);
2019: }

(End of file - total 2019 lines)
</content>

state.title: packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts

parts.text:
- **Medium — Untrusted `localCochange` data is persisted into shared co-change cache**
  - In `packages/worker/src/lib/review-runner.ts:1069` and `packages/worker/src/lib/review-runner.ts:1083`, the worker accepts `provenance.localCochange` from the request and writes it directly via `upsertReviewCochangeCacheBatch`.
  - The cache lookup/write key is only `(repo, file_path)` (see `packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts:3267` and `packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts:3330`), so this can overwrite canonical GitHub-derived cache rows for later reviews.
  - Real failure mode: any caller that can submit a review request can inject arbitrary related paths/frequencies for a repo, and subsequent reviews may read poisoned co-change context and rank unrelated files, degrading review quality in a persistent way.

- **Low — Invalid `localCochange` still changes idempotency behavior**
  - `buildReviewRequestPayload` only includes normalized `localCochange` when valid (`packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:357`), but `hasExtendedReviewIdempotencyInputs` treats any object-shaped `provenance.localCochange` as “extended” (`packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:558`).
  - Real failure mode: a malformed `localCochange` object (that gets dropped during normalization) can still disable legacy idempotency-hash aliasing, causing unexpected `409 idempotency_key_conflict` for requests that are otherwise semantically identical.
